title: Packaging material conveyance system having air ducts
abstract: A packaging material conveyance system is configured to convey protective packaging articles including pads made of paper dunnage from a protective packaging machine to a receptacle. A main duct receives the articles output by the protective packaging machine and transports the articles to the receptacle. The articles are conveyed by an airflow generated by a blower that propels the articles through the main duct, and are deposited into the receptacle after being released through an outlet. The outlet may be associated with a diverter that forms part of the main duct in a closed position, and is configured to release the articles into the receptacle in an open position.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A packaging material conveyance system for conveying protective packaging articles from a protective packaging machine to a receptacle, the packaging material conveyance system comprising: a main duct having a packaging article inlet configured to receive formed packaging articles, and having a first outlet configured to allow the packaging articles to exit therefrom, the main duct being elongated and configured to transport the articles in a longitudinal direction within the main duct from the packaging article inlet to the first outlet; and a blower assembly operably connected to the main duct to generate airflow that enters the main duct from at least two non-adjacent sides of the main duct to propel the articles through the main duct. 2. The conveyance system of claim 1, wherein the packaging article inlet is connected to an outlet of the protective packaging machine that converts a high-density supply material into the packaging articles having a lower density than the supply material. 3. The conveyance system of claim 1, wherein the receptacle is arranged at the first outlet, and configured to receive the articles conveyed by an internal duct airflow from the packaging article inlet to the first outlet. 4. The conveyance system of claim 1 , further comprising a blower duct section fluidly connecting the blower assembly and the main duct to introduce the airflow at an angle relative to the longitudinal direction of the main duct. 5. The conveyance system of claim 4, wherein the blower duct section comprises first and second blower duct sections, and the airflow comprises first and second air streams configured to enter the main duct via the first and second blower duct sections at first and second angles, respectively. 6. The conveyance system of claim 5, wherein the first and second angles are the same. 7. The conveyance system of claim 6, wherein the first and second angles are not greater than about 90Â°. 8. The conveyance system of claim 6, wherein the first and second angles are not greater than about 60Â°. 9. The conveyance system of claim 5, wherein the first and second air streams are configured to energize boundary layers on top and bottom sides of the articles, respectively, so as to accelerate the articles from the packaging article inlet of the main duct. 10. The conveyance system of claim 5, wherein the first and second air streams are configured to engage opposite sides of the articles. 11. The conveyance system of claim 5, wherein the blower comprises a first blower for generating the first air stream and a second blower for generating the second air stream. 12. The conveyance system of claim 11, wherein the first blower and the second blower are arranged on the at least two non-adjacent sides of the main duct. 13. The conveyance system of claim 12, wherein the first and second blowers are arranged proximate the packaging article inlet of the main duct. 14. The conveyance system of claim 1, wherein the main duct comprises first and second duct sections, the first and second duct sections each defining a substantially straight path. 15. The conveyance system of claim 14, wherein the first and second duct sections are rectangular in shape. 16. The conveyance system of claim 14, wherein the first duct section is generally horizontal and defines a change in elevation. 17. The conveyance system of claim 14, further comprising an elbow of the main duct arranged between the first and second duct sections, the elbow being wider than the first and second duct sections. 18. The conveyance system of claim 1, wherein the main duct is configured to receive the articles at the packaging article inlet that have been cut and separated by the protective packaging machine. 19. The conveyance system of claim 18, wherein the articles are paper dunnage pads. 20. The conveyance system of claim 19, wherein the pads are two-ply paper pads. 21. A packaging material conveyance system for conveying protective packaging articles from a protective packaging machine to a receptacle, the packaging material conveyance system comprising: a main duct extending a predetermined length from a packaging article inlet to at least a first outlet, the main duct configured to receive the articles output by the protective packaging machine and transport the articles to a first outlet corresponding to the receptacle, the main duct defining a longitudinal direction corresponding to a direction of travel of the articles in the main duct; a first blower operably connected to a first blower duct section for generating a first airflow that enters the main duct at a first predetermined angle relative to the longitudinal direction; and 

 a second blower operably connected to a second blower duct section for generating a second airflow that enters the main duct at a second predetermined angle relative to the longitudinal direction, wherein the first and second air streams are configured to propel the articles through the main duct. 22. The conveyance system of claim 21, wherein the packaging article inlet is connected to an outlet of the protective packaging machine that converts a high-density supply material into the packaging articles having a lower density than the supply material. 23. The conveyance system of claim 21, wherein the receptacle is arranged at the first outlet, and configured to receive the articles conveyed by an internal duct airflow from the packaging article inlet to the first outlet. 24. The conveyance system of claim 21, further comprising first and second blower duct sections fluidly connecting the first and second blower assemblies, respectively, and the main duct to introduce the first and second airflows at first and second angles, respectively, relative to the longitudinal direction of the main duct. 25. The conveyance system of claim 24, wherein the first and second airflows are configured to enter the main duct via the first and second blower duct sections at the first and second angles, respectively. 26. The conveyance system of claim 25, wherein the first and second angles are the same. 27. The conveyance system of claim 26, wherein the first and second angles are not greater than about 90Â°. 28. The conveyance system of claim 26, wherein the first and second angles are not greater than about 60Â°. 29. The conveyance system of claim 25, wherein the first and second airflows are configured to energize boundary layers on top and bottom sides of the articles, respectively, so as to accelerate the articles from the packaging article inlet of the main duct. 30. The conveyance system of claim 25, wherein the first and second airflows are configured to engage opposite sides of the articles. 31. The conveyance system of claim 21, wherein the first blower and the second blower are arranged on the at least two non-adjacent sides of the main duct. 32. The conveyance system of claim 21, wherein the first and second blowers are arranged proximate the packaging article inlet of the main duct. 33. The conveyance system of claim 21, wherein the main duct comprises first and second duct sections, the first and second duct sections each defining a substantially straight path. 34. The conveyance system of claim 33, wherein the first and second duct sections are rectangular in shape. 35. The conveyance system of claim 33, wherein the first duct section is generally horizontal and defines a change in elevation. 36. The conveyance system of claim 33, further comprising an elbow of the main duct arranged between the first and second duct sections, the elbow being wider than the first and second duct sections. 37. The conveyance system of claim 21, wherein the main duct is configured to receive the articles at the packaging article inlet that have been cut and separated by the protective packaging machine. 38. The conveyance system of claim 37, wherein the articles are paper dunnage pads. 39. The conveyance system of claim 38, wherein the pads are two-ply paper pads. 40. A packaging material conveyance system for conveying protective packaging articles from a protective packaging machine to a receptacle, the packaging material conveyance system comprising: 

 a main duct extending a predetermined length from a packaging article inlet to at least a diverter, the main duct configured to receive the articles output by the protective packaging machine and transport the articles to the diverter corresponding to the receptacle, the main duct defining a longitudinal direction corresponding to a direction of travel of the articles in the main duct; the diverter being configured to act as an outlet in an open position and as part of the main duct in a closed position, wherein in the open position the packaging articles are diverted into the receptacle and when in the closed position the packaging articles are configured to continue to an additional receptacle that follows the receptacle; a blower operably connected to the main duct, the blower oriented at an angle to the longitudinal direction of the main duct so as to generate airflow to propel the articles through the main duct; and the receptacle associated configured to receive the articles conveyed by the internal duct airflow from the inlet to the diverter. 41. The packaging material conveyance system of claim 40, wherein a portion of the diverter is configured to rest within a notch when in the open position. 42. The conveyance system of claim 40, wherein the packaging article inlet is connected to an outlet of the protective packaging machine that converts a high-density supply material into the packaging articles having a lower density than the supply material. 43. The conveyance system of claim 40, wherein the receptacle is arranged at the diverter, and configured to receive the articles conveyed by an internal duct airflow from the packaging article inlet to the diverter. 44. The conveyance system of claim 40, further comprising a blower duct section fluidly connecting the blower and the main duct to introduce the airflow at an angle relative to the longitudinal direction of the main duct. 45. The conveyance system of claim 44, wherein the blower comprises first and second blower duct sections, and the airflow comprises first and second air streams configured to enter the main duct via the first and second blower duct sections at first and second angles, respectively. 46. The conveyance system of claim 45, wherein the first and second angles are the same. 47. The conveyance system of claim 46, wherein the first and second angles are not greater than about 90Â°. 48. The conveyance system of claim 46, wherein the first and second angles are not greater than about 60Â°. 49. The conveyance system of claim 45, wherein the first and second air streams are configured to energize boundary layers on top and bottom sides of the articles, respectively, so as to accelerate the articles from the packaging article inlet of the main duct. 50. The conveyance system of claim 45, wherein the first and second air streams are configured to engage opposite sides of the articles. 51. The conveyance system of claim 55, wherein the blower comprises a first blower for generating the first air stream and a second blower for generating the second air stream. 52. The conveyance system of claim 51, wherein the first blower and the second blower are arranged on the at least two non-adjacent sides of the main duct. 53. The conveyance system of claim 52, wherein the first and second blowers are arranged proximate the packaging article inlet of the main duct. 54. The conveyance system of claim 51, wherein the main duct comprises first and second duct sections, the first and second duct sections each defining a substantially straight path. 55. The conveyance system of claim 54, wherein the first and second duct sections are rectangular in shape. 56. The conveyance system of claim 54, wherein the first duct section is generally horizontal and defines a change in elevation. 57. The conveyance system of claim 54, further comprising an elbow of the main duct arranged between the first and second duct sections, the elbow being wider than the first and second duct sections. 58. The conveyance system of claim 40, wherein the main duct is configured to receive the articles at the packaging article inlet that have been cut and separated by the protective packaging machine. 59. The conveyance system of claim 58, wherein the articles are paper dunnage pads. 60. The conveyance system of claim 59, wherein the pads are two-ply paper pads.
